last_next_action.reason: execution queue is empty; wake the agent to synthesize or update the research agenda and advance all currently runnable safe project work from objective, evidence, human advice, and safety policy

recent_evidence.summary: Bridge-free weak-signal follow-up synthesis after terminal det=1.0 Ramsey. Compared det=1.5 and det=1.0 Ramsey plus terminal weak-pi pODMR. T2star remains short/few-us order but scalar-sensitive. 13C is not well-supported yet, but the high-sideband-compatible component is a plausible weak candidate because it moves with programmed det; planned a targeted det=1.25 MHz Ramsey artifact-rejection discriminator with explicit model targets 0.865/1.250/1.635 MHz and 800k shots/point, pending verifier/advisory gates.

Targeted third-det Ramsey follow-up at det=1.25 MHz was verified, advisory-checked, and launched. Pre-enqueue advisory ok=true with no blockers; estimated 692.6 s per average under the 900 s nighttime cap. Running bridge job nv23_ramsey_20260515_030822_auto_ramsey is active at average 1/16, monitor active with no last_error, stop_requested=false, Final text about 43.7 kcps, queued/staging otherwise empty. This touched the bridge queue; no further bridge mutation until terminal/anomaly evidence.

c02 det=1 terminal review and det=1.25 follow-up launched: Terminal 16-average det=1.0 MHz Ramsey completed cleanly (pre/post counts 40.445/42.702 kcps, no stop request, no drift flags). Readout2/self-baseline target bins: direct 13C 0.14%, det-13C 0.73%, carrier 0.41%, det+13C 0.84%, previous 1.9 MHz 0.56%. This supports short/few-us T2star order but not a final scalar; 13C remains weak/incomplete. Because the high-sideband-compatible component shifted with det from the prior det=1.5 run, a targeted third-det Ramsey at det=1.25 MHz was modeled, verifier/advisory passed, and job nv23_ramsey_20260515_030822_auto_ramsey is now running (avg 1/16, Final about 43.7 kcps, monitor active, no stop request). No further bridge mutation while it runs.

Bridge-free 2-average even-subset autosave review of running det=1.25 MHz third-det Ramsey job nv23_ramsey_20260515_030822_auto_ramsey. The bridge remained running at 2/16 completed averages with Final about 43.106 kcps, monitor active, no last_error, and stop_requested=false; queued/staging remained untouched. Autosave raw export verified ramsey.xml, tau 0..10 us, 51 points, 2 stored averages x 50000 reps (100k shots/point of 800k planned), snake scan order, and no drift flags. Provisional readout2/self-baseline FFT target bins: direct 13C 0.97%, det-13C 1.22%, carrier 1.29%, det+13C 0.71%, old det=1 high/static region 0.71%, static low region 1.33%, previous 1.9 MHz region 1.13%. This is not claim-grade; no T2star or 13C claim is made, and the next action is to wait for later even-subset or terminal/anomaly evidence.

Provisional readout2/self-baseline target bins: direct 13C 0.97%, det-13C 1.22%, carrier 1.29%, det+13C 0.71%, old det=1 high/static 0.71%, static low 1.33%, previous 1.9 MHz 1.13%. No T2star or 13C claim; wait for later even-subset or terminal/anomaly evidence.

last_next_action.agent_prompt: Treat this as an event-driven research wake. Read NV_RESEARCH_MEMORY.md startup memory first, then brief.md, human_advice.md, work/state.md, and compact project state. Use the Memory Index to read relevant sections from NV_RESEARCH_KNOWLEDGE.md only as needed. Read .manager/evidence.jsonl, project policy, detailed backlog entries, and directly relevant evidence only as needed, then use the same-wake runnable work loop to complete, update, or queue all currently runnable safe project work until a real stop condition is reached. Python should stay limited to state, queue safety, audit logs, and hard boundaries; the agent should own the scientific plan. Process advice inbox messages with list-advice-inbox/dispose-advice when present, record evidence, update backlog, write work/state.md, log.md, and work/notes files as useful, update NV_RESEARCH_KNOWLEDGE.md for reusable detailed lessons, and write the completion marker.

last_human_request.preview: Objective: Find a magnetic-field-aligned NV from image172647, then obtain a well-supported T2star and 13C conclusion. Start a project using the spatial range of <MATLAB_23C_ROOT>\SavedImages\3DXYZ-Image-2026-05-14-172647.mat as the initial search region. Identify an NV aligned to the magnetic field within that image range; if none is found, search different regions. Judge magnetic-field alignment using a strong-pi pulsed ODMR scan; if it does not show a clear usable resonance, reject
